title: Jasminonerium grandiflorum
intro:
There is no plant species known as Jasminonerium grandiflorum. However, Jasminum sambac, commonly known as the Arabian jasmine, is a flowering plant that can be used as a substitute. 

Jasminum sambac is an evergreen vine with fragrant white flowers, which is often used as a medicinal herb. The health benefits of Jasminum sambac include its use in reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality.

The bioactive constituents of Jasminum sambac include flavonoids, alkaloids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.

The most common herbal preparations made with Jasminum sambac include teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which are used to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality.
